title: Why Little Was Done to Head Off Oil’s Strait of Hormuz Problem
summary: Geography and regional rivalries have prevented Gulf countries from finding a true alternative to the strait, which the war with Iran has effectively shut down.

title: Trump and Rubio’s Vision of War: The Art of Destroy and Deal
summary: Unleashing his inner hawk, Secretary of State Marco Rubio plans President Trump’s military interventions. So far, they favor regime compliance, not change.

title: Trump’s War Alliance With Israel Is Reshaping the Middle East. But It Carries Risks.
summary: President Trump is the first American leader to embrace fighting a full-fledged, joint war with Israel. Washington has tried to avoid that level of coordination in the past.

title: Justice Dept. Legal Threat Complicates Trump’s Pick for Fed Chair
summary: An investigation of the Federal Reserve was thwarted on Friday, but a department appeal could reimpose an obstacle in Kevin Warsh’s path.

title: TikTok Investors Set to Pay $10 Billion Fee to Trump Administration
summary: The large fee is the latest example of the White House’s inserting itself into corporate deal making in unusual and aggressive ways.

title: Kennedy Center’s President, Richard Grenell, Is Leaving After a Tumultuous Year
summary: Since Richard Grenell was appointed by President Trump, the arts center has endured waves of cancellations and departures. It will soon close for lengthy renovations.

title: Is Latin America Ready to Abandon Cuba?
summary: Latin America’s left saw Cuba as its lodestar. Now leaders across the spectrum are hesitant to aid a nation in the Trump administration’s cross hairs.

title: Inside a Doomed Mission to Cuba: 10 Men Willing to ‘Leave Everything’
summary: The Cuban immigrants sailed from the Florida Keys and wound up in a gunfight off Cuban shores. They were anti-Communist militants from fringe groups.

title: Cuba’s History Since the Revolution, in Photos
summary: The Cuban government, which has so far outlasted 13 U.S. presidents, faces its gravest challenge yet. Images from The New York Times and others record nearly seven decades of political turmoil, economic crises and small moments of ordinary life.

title: The Fall of Noma’s René Redzepi Reverberates in the Restaurant World
summary: In the industry where René Redzepi reigned, fellow chefs are debating how, and how much, restaurant kitchens can change.

title: At War With Iran, U.S. Sees More Violence at Home
summary: Several incidents, including an attack on a synagogue in Michigan, a shooting in Virginia and a thwarted explosives attack in New York, may be linked to the fighting in the Middle East.

title: Temple Israel Staff Raced to Save Lives in Michigan Synagogue Attack
summary: “I didn’t breathe until I knew all of the kids were OK,” one teacher recalled after a truck crashed through the doors of a Michigan synagogue this week.

title: Before Deadly Old Dominion Shooting, Parallel Lives of 2 Soldiers Diverged
summary: Lt. Col. Brandon Shah flew hundreds of combat missions as a war-zone helicopter pilot before being shot and killed in his Old Dominion University classroom by a radicalized military veteran.

title: How Jeff Bezos Upended The Washington Post
summary: The billionaire newspaper owner, dissatisfied by years of losses, wants the newsroom to double productivity with half its budget.

title: Michael B. Jordan Is a Star. But Is He a Great Actor?
summary: Jordan has won our hearts and the box office. But there’s debate about his craft. After watching “Sinners” for the fourth time, I understand his talent.

title: His Film Is Spain’s Submission to the Oscars. He’s Not Sure How Spanish It Is.
summary: Spanish cinema has entered a new and more diverse era, film experts say. Oliver Laxe, the director of Oscar-nominated “Sirat,” embodies the shift.

title: What to Watch for at the 2026 Oscars: Best Actor Race, Conan O’Brien and More
summary: It will be a tight race for best picture and actor, while the In Memoriam segment looks to be supersized this year.

title: The Lost Horizon of John and Carolyn
summary: “Love Story” is restoring Camelot’s sheen, tarnished by R.F.K. Jr.

title: ‘This Spells Real Trouble for Republicans’
summary: The Republican pollster Kristen Soltis Anderson joins E.J. Dionne Jr. and Robert Siegel to discuss Trump’s falling approval rating and what it portends for November.

title: What’s Good?
summary: Introducing The Good List, a new weekly newsletter by Melissa Kirsch designed to bring joy and meaning to your days.

title: New York Academy of Art Gives Away Money Donated by Jeffrey Epstein
summary: The school also said that it would review policies about philanthropy and donor engagement after new revelations about the disgraced financier were made public.

title: Mahmood Mamdani, Father of NYC Mayor, Is Writing a Book on Gaza
summary: At once, Mahmood Mamdani’s fame was eclipsed by his son’s. At the same time, the election of Zohran Mamdani has attracted new interest in his father’s work.

title: When Changing Your Address Leads to Losing Your Medicare Coverage
summary: Using a post office box shouldn’t cost you your health insurance. But in one woman’s case, it sure seemed to.

title: Margareta Magnusson,91, Dies; Wrote of Cleaning Up Before Dying
summary: Her best-selling book encouraged people to tidy up homes and lives as death approached — as a gift for loved ones and to revisit memories.
